execSummary: **MAHA ELEVATE Accelerator due May 5 (T+2 days)** — Linda leads writing; Stefan owes the cross-grant Word doc to Eli by Monday so Eli can guide ROI/SERA/RMG budget paths.

Funding runway only confirmed through October (Eli); team scoping to stretch to November because December is naturally idle. Hiring posture is fixed-term RC (4-month tour) per Dalia, not volunteer interns.

**Website is the chokepoint.** Mona at Roses Lab and other potential interns/collaborators bounce off NOURISH because there's no public site. The new directive is 4-week sprint to a v1 they can be 'happy with.'
Cuisine-based / taste-forward repositioning is the single biggest message change in 6 months. Every artifact (placemat, ADA pitch, video series, About page) needs language audit before going public.
**ADA partnership stalled on rewrite request** — they reject the block-format placemat and want a rewrite. Dalia owns; Mehek+Stephanie were original drafters.
Razor (Dustin) consulting deal is governance-blocked — Stanford OTL Chelsea says they don't broker consulting agreements. Don't reveal Stefan's hand; Dr. Moharir will request follow-up first.

Ankita Patel raised ~$3M for medical-misinformation startup partnered with AHA. Strategic fit is 'community-oriented misinformation' — too early for exact collab; right move is intro deck + 3-way call.

Dr. Rosas's intern Mona is researching MTM reimbursement pathways; 12-month ROI window bottleneck. CGM glycemic-marker outcome shortens horizon — possible Sous angle but MD-billing constraint blocks it.
South Asian cohort: Cupertino obesity-medicine partner offers 9 incremental patients + 4 current. Filipino cohort target = 15 (or fallback 5+5).

**Stefan re-onboarding to NSW disability policy team** — Friday return at Surry Hills, 1.5 days/week. NDRP admin ($13.7M ending Dec 2027), NDIS outcomes review (Mark), disability research (Louisa Ratz).
Stanford Health Library wins as canonical NOURISH video host — better analytics + clinical credibility than AEM. Pitch sequence: Nicole Scandlyn → Lisa Kim → Maya Adam → SoM editors.
Stefan is **47 NOURISH tasks overdue or Not Started** in Task Registry, including pre-March commitments (VMED Poster, Stanford Continuing Ed, ADA placemat). Recommend 90-min triage block this week.
Razor + Granpadilla + Dr. Rosas + ICO + Isabella + Sylvia (P0 intro doc) are 6 stalled relationships. Each will burn social capital if not touched this week — 5 short '~3 line' emails on Monday.
Eli is the gating relationship for funding ops — he wants visibility on every grant before the team commits time. The Word doc is the single most leveraged deliverable Stefan can ship this week.

Sous V1 + craving-parser is starting to look like an under-leveraged research asset for grants. The Sous food-recognition + CGM + behavior data combo is exactly what NSF Smart Health, Google.org, and AHRQ R21 fund.

Linda's PCORI LOI submission unblocks $1M cardiovascular comparative-effectiveness path — and she pulled Le Cordon Bleu chefs into NOURISH plating principles. Cross-pollinating culinary expertise into Sous is a sleeper differentiator.

Add 1-line description per grant. Save 'NOURISH Grants Pipeline — May 2026.docx', email Eli with subject 'NOURISH grants pipeline overview as you requested.'

grants.name: RWJF — From Insight to Action: Health Equity Research that Meets This Moment
grants.match: **Why Stefan can win:** RWJF explicitly seeks community-rooted research that 'values cultural and community knowledge as equally important' — NOURISH's API-population dietary intervention is exactly that thesis. **Asset:** 408K-view public-health talk demonstrates community reach + 2+ years of API community partnerships. **Aim 1:** Community-engaged CER comparing culturally-tailored Sous coaching vs. generic dietary education for T2D risk reduction in South Asian/Filipino adults, primary outcome A1C and Mediterranean-diet adherence at 6 months. **Risk:** Co-PI must be a community-based-organization leader — confirm Cupertino partner or Filipino community contact will sign as co-PI BEFORE LOI submission.

grants.name: HHS MAHA ELEVATE — PCPH AI Nutrition Counseling Add-On
grants.amount: Add-on track to PCPH digital infrastructure base award
grants.match: **Why Stefan can win:** NORA (meal-pairer AI) is operational; PCPH Stanford partnership gives clinician deployment site — the rare combination ELEVATE wants. **Aim 1:** Embed NORA-driven culturally-tailored nutrition counseling into a Stanford PCPH primary-care workflow for prediabetes patients; measure clinician adoption rate, patient A1C delta, time-per-encounter. **Risk:** MAHA add-on requires MAHA-Commission-aligned framing (chronic-disease-prevention via lifestyle) — align Aim 1 narrative to MAHA pillars before submitting.

grants.name: NIH PRIMED-AI — Translating AI/ML Models to the Clinic (RFA-RM-26-013)
grants.funder: NIH Common Fund (Office of the Director) — PRIMED-AI
grants.amount: Up to ~$1.5M direct cost over 4 years (cooperative agreement)
grants.match: **Why Stefan can win:** PRIMED-AI explicitly targets translation of validated AI models into clinical workflows — NOURISH has NORA (clinical-grade meal-pairer) + Stanford PCPH deployment site. **Aim 1:** Translate NORA from research-validated to clinically-deployed nutrition-counseling CDS in Stanford PCPH primary care; measure clinician adoption, patient outcomes, workflow integration. **Risk:** Cooperative agreement = active NIH program-staff involvement; identify PRIMED-AI program officer and initiate contact BEFORE May 18 LOI.

grants.name: Ardmore Institute of Health — Culinary Medicine Teaching Kitchen Grant
grants.match: **Why Stefan can win:** Ardmore funded Dr. Linda Koh’s prior teaching-kitchen work — warm precedent. **Asset:** PBDI Teaching Kitchen curriculum already developed; Koh has the publication record (Frontiers in Nutrition 2024, JAMA Network Open) Ardmore reviewers want to see. **Aim 1:** RCT of culturally-tailored teaching-kitchen vs. standard nutrition education for South Asian and Filipino adults at T2D risk; primary outcome 6-month dietary adherence + A1C. **Risk:** Ardmore favors established teaching-kitchen sites — must name a confirmed kitchen partner (Cantor PBDI kitchen or Koh’s UMass site) in proposal. **Owner: Dr. Linda Koh.**

grants.name: ADA Pathway to Stop Diabetes — Accelerator Award
grants.match: **Why Stefan can win:** Pathway Accelerator targets mid-career investigators with novel high-impact diabetes ideas — culturally-tailored digital diabetes prevention is an underserved Pathway theme. **Aim 1:** Develop and validate Sous AI-coach as a 5-year longitudinal intervention for prediabetes-to-T2D conversion prevention in API populations. **Risk:** Pathway is competitive and biased toward bench science — must frame Sous as a behavioral mechanism (decision-support → dietary-pattern shift → metabolic outcome) rather than a tech demo.

grants.name: NIDDK Small R01s for Clinical Trials (PAS-25-102)
grants.match: **Why Stefan can win:** Small R01 mechanism is the right size for a focused CGM-Sous pilot trial in API populations. **Aim 1:** Single-site RCT (Stanford) of Sous AI-coach + CGM vs. CGM-alone for prediabetes T2D conversion prevention in South Asian adults; primary outcome A1C + dietary-pattern adherence at 6 months. **Risk:** 4-day window to deadline; must have IRB-approved protocol or skip this cycle and target next R01 standard date (June 5).

grants.name: AHRQ Digital Healthcare Solutions R21/R33
grants.match: **Why Stefan can win:** NOURISH CGM cohort (n=60, 2 ethnicities) is the AHRQ-favored 'point-of-care digital tool with embedded outcome data' phenotype. **Aim 1:** RCT of Sous AI-coach app vs. usual care for newly-diagnosed T2D in API populations; primary outcome HbA1c at 12 weeks. **Risk:** AHRQ wants implementation plan more than novelty — commit to two-site rollout (Stanford PCPH + Cupertino partner) in cover letter.

grants.name: PCORI Phased Large Awards for Comparative Effectiveness Research — Cycle 2 2026
grants.amount: Phase 1: $1M (1 yr planning); Phase 2: up to $20M (4 yr trial)
grants.match: **Why Stefan can win:** Phased Large mechanism rewards CER teams ready for a national pragmatic trial. **Aim 1:** National-scale CER comparing Sous AI-coach vs. usual primary-care nutrition counseling for T2D-risk reduction across 10–20 sites in API-dense regions. **Risk:** LOI window CLOSED Apr 28 — if not submitted, pivot to Cycle 1 2027 (LOI typically Jan 2027). If submitted, await June 2 status notification.

grants.name: NIH Dissemination and Implementation Research in Health (PAR-25-144) R01
grants.amount: R01 standard ($500K–$2.5M direct cost over 5 yr)
grants.deadline: Standard NIH R01 dates (Feb 5, Jun 5, Oct 5 annually)
grants.match: **Why Stefan can win:** D&I R01 is the natural follow-on to AHRQ R21/R33 — fund the implementation arm of Sous-in-clinic deployment. **Aim 1:** Implementation-effectiveness hybrid trial measuring uptake, fidelity, cost, and clinician acceptance of NORA in Stanford PCPH (vs. unassisted clinician baseline). **Risk:** D&I R01 requires partnered evaluator team — confirm Russ Glasgow / Borsika Rabin (CU Anschutz) or Tracy Finch (UK) collaboration before submission.

grants.name: PCORI Broad Pragmatic Studies — Cycle 2 2026
grants.match: **Why Stefan can win:** Diabetes Prevention/Care/Treatment is a named Special Area of Emphasis for Cycle 2 2026 — direct fit. **Aim 1:** Pragmatic trial of teaching-kitchen + Sous coaching vs. printed nutrition education in primary-care prediabetes patients across PCPH + 2 partner clinics; primary outcome A1C + 6-month dietary adherence. **Risk:** LOI window CLOSED Apr 28 — same status pathway as GR-024.

grants.name: HRSA FY2026 Expanding Nutrition Services (ENS) — $125M
grants.amount: Up to $125M total tranche; per-applicant award TBD (typical $250K–$1M for FQHC-affiliated)
grants.match: **Why Stefan can win:** ENS funds direct nutrition services in FQHC-affiliated networks — NOURISH cookbook + teaching kitchen + Sous app together form a deployable nutrition-services package. **Aim 1:** Deploy NOURISH stack at 3 FQHCs serving API populations in Bay Area + Hawaii; measure utilization rate, patient-reported dietary change, clinician time-savings. **Risk:** Eligibility limited to active H80 award holders; must subcontract under an established FQHC prime — identify Bay Area FQHC partner (Asian Health Services Oakland?) before LOI.

grants.name: NIH RFA-DK-26-315 — Digital Health Technology for Type 2 Diabetes Management
grants.amount: Up to $1.5M total cost across 3 awards in FY26 + $3M/year FY27–30
grants.match: **Why Stefan can win:** RFA-DK-26-315 names CGM + telemedicine for remote T2D management as priority — NOURISH CGM cohort + Sous + NORA stack maps directly. **Aim 1:** RCT testing multimodal digital intervention (CGM + Sous + NORA) vs. CGM-only for T2D management in newly-diagnosed API patients; primary outcome A1C at 6 months. **Risk:** R01 mechanism requires preliminary feasibility data — use the rest of 2026 to publish a CGM cohort feasibility paper as bridge evidence.

grants.name: NIH Intervention Research to Improve Native American Health (IRINAH) R34
grants.amount: Up to $450K direct cost over 3 years
grants.match: **Why Stefan can win:** IRINAH R34 funds intervention development for Native American/Indigenous populations — NOURISH PFEME Nepal arm extends naturally to Indigenous food-systems framing. **Aim 1:** Co-develop a culturally-grounded indigenous-food nutrition curriculum with Nepal partner network; pilot dietary-diversity-score change in maternal/child cohort. **Risk:** R34 is intervention-development-only; cannot fund efficacy trial. Position as pre-R01 feasibility, with explicit roadmap to R01.

grants.name: NIDDK High Impact Interdisciplinary Science (RC2)
grants.match: **Why Stefan can win:** RC2 mechanism rewards risky-but-potentially-paradigm-shifting interdisciplinary work — culturally-tailored AI dietary coaching for API T2D prevention is underexplored at NIH. **Aim 1:** Build and validate the first culturally-adaptive digital-twin metabolic model for API populations using the NOURISH CGM cohort. **Risk:** RC2 favors established PIs; pair with senior co-PI (Christopher Gardner or Latha Palaniappan) for credibility.

grants.name: CMS Rural Health Transformation (RHT) — Technology Catalyst Fund
grants.amount: $50B federal pool FY26–30; per-vendor $500K–$20M depending on state
grants.match: **Why Stefan can win:** Single largest new federal digital-health funding stream this decade; nutrition-based chronic-disease prevention is named priority. **Asset:** Sous + NORA + CGM bundle is exactly the consumer + clinical digital-health stack RHT seeks. **Aim 1:** Deploy NOURISH stack in [State X]'s rural FQHC network; measure 12-month A1C reduction + total cost of care. **Risk:** New-vendor risk is the #1 rejection factor — subcontract under state-Medicaid prime (Maximus, Gainwell, Accenture, Deloitte State Health) before approaching states directly.

ideas.title: The Grandmother Hypothesis of Nutrition Science
ideas.essay: Every food scientist ignores grandmothers. **That's the mistake.** Stefan's NOURISH cohort + FlavorDB access + CGM trial infrastructure make him the first person who could actually validate this claim with controlled data: traditional cuisines encode generations of glycemic-response selection that modern dietetic dogma never measured.
ideas.mechanism: Cluster traditional South Asian + Filipino recipe corpora using FlavorDB molecular signatures, run them through the existing CGM cohort, compare predicted vs. observed glycemic AUC; publish the deltas as 'inherited culinary wisdom that beats RDN-prescribed plates.'
seeds: South Asian + Filipino recipe corpus from current study
ideas.impact: Very High — Nature Food paper + viral essay
ideas.edge: Stefan is uniquely positioned: he has the cohort data + the food chemistry + the AI pipeline + the cultural credibility from the NOURISH framing. Nobody else has all four.
ideas.claudePrompt: Draft a 1,500-word essay titled 'The Grandmother Hypothesis of Nutrition Science.' Open with the 2-sentence hook above. Argue: traditional cuisines encode selection pressure for glycemic-friendly meals; modern dietetics threw this out; Stefan's data lets us measure the gap. Include 3 specific examples from NOURISH CGM data. Close with the call to fund a 'Cultural Glycemic Atlas' project.

ideas.title: FlavorDB × CGM = the missing pairing engine
ideas.essay: Sous already does cuisine-aware pairing. Layer FlavorDB molecular vectors on top + CGM observed responses, and you get a deterministic engine that predicts glycemic impact from recipe text alone. This is what Google.org and NSF Smart Health are funding for $500K–$1.2M each.
ideas.mechanism: Encode each recipe as FlavorDB molecule vector → train regressor on CGM AUC labels from cohort → ship API as research-by-default. Repurpose Sous craving-parser as the recipe ingestion front-end.
ideas.edge: Stefan owns the only consumer-facing app that's already FlavorDB-integrated AND has CGM ground-truth.
ideas.claudePrompt: Open Cursor scaffold for Next.js + Python service. Python: FastAPI /predict that takes recipe text, calls Sous craving-parser → ingredient list → FlavorDB lookup → simple feedforward predictor for AUC. Train on placeholder CGM labels CSV. Wire Sous frontend to show predicted AUC alongside recipe. Save in /sous/labs/glycemic-predictor/.

ideas.title: The CGM Sommelier — pair wines to glucose curves
ideas.essay: Wine pairing today is purely sensory. Stefan's NOURISH CGM data + Mike-the-winery-manager pipeline (via Linda) is the perfect test bed for a sommelier model that pairs wines to predicted glucose response. Wine industry hasn't been hit by digital biomarkers yet; this is greenfield.
ideas.mechanism: Run plant-forward charcuterie + wine pairing dinner Linda already proposed, instrument every guest with a CGM, cluster pairings by glucose response, train small model. Publish as viral 'wines that don't spike you' guide.
seeds: Mike (Saratoga winery manager) intro from Linda
Plant-forward charcuterie event Dr. Moharir already discussed
ideas.edge: Wine industry has no digital biomarker reflex. Nutrition industry has no wine reflex. Stefan sits at the only intersection.
ideas.claudePrompt: Outline 3-screen mobile site: (1) wine intake form + CGM upload, (2) live AUC chart with wine label, (3) ranking of all wines tested. Use Tailwind + Recharts. Save to /sous/labs/cgm-sommelier/.

ideas.title: Misinformation as a public-health pairing problem
ideas.essay: Ankita Patel raised $3M to fight medical misinformation. Misinformation isn't a content problem — it's a *pairing* problem. People believe whichever claim shows up next to a trusted face. NOURISH + Ankita's startup can A/B-test pairing strategies across statin + nutrition content and ship the first IRB-grade misinformation pairing engine.
ideas.mechanism: Treat content + influencer pairs as recommender problem. Use NOURISH cohort as population. Outcome: belief change at 30 days. Publish + grant pitch.
ideas.edge: Stefan owns the pairing-engine intellectual stack from food science. Translating to misinformation is a 1-year sprint.
ideas.claudePrompt: Draft 2-page joint research proposal between NOURISH and Ankita's startup: 'Pairing Theory of Misinformation Resistance.' Title + 1 paragraph background + 3 aims + 1 figure outline + 5-line budget rationale. Focus on statins as the test case.

ideas.title: MTM reimbursement shortcut via CGM glycemic ROI
ideas.essay: Mona at Roses Lab is solving a 12-month MTM reimbursement ROI problem. CGM glycemic markers compress that ROI window to ~30 days. Bottleneck is MD-billing for monitoring. Fix is a population-health structure where MDs sign off in batches.
ideas.mechanism: Design 'Glycemic ROI MTM' regulatory wrapper: MD attestation + CGM data + Sous coaching, billed as population health intervention with CMS RHT Technology Catalyst alignment. This is the GR-080 pool.
seeds: GR-080 CMS RHT Technology Catalyst $50B pool
ideas.edge: Stefan has CGM + MD network + AI coaching stack. MTM providers don't. MTM providers have meal supply chain. The handshake is the value.
ideas.claudePrompt: Draft 1-page concept memo titled 'CGM-anchored MTM ROI: 30 days, not 12 months.' Audience: Dr. Rosas + Stefan + Eli. Sections: problem, fix, evidence ask, asks. Save to wiki/nourish/concepts/.

ideas.title: The Cultural Glycemic Atlas — open dataset
ideas.essay: Right now nutrition data is locked behind RCTs and proprietary apps. NOURISH cohort + Stefan's open-by-default disposition makes a Cultural Glycemic Atlas inevitable: ethnic cuisine × CGM response, public, citable. Funders love public goods.
ideas.mechanism: De-identify CGM cohort meal+response pairs, anchor each meal to cuisine label + FlavorDB profile, publish on Hugging Face + Stanford Digital Repository. Pair with paper.
ideas.edge: First mover. Nobody else has cohort + cuisine breadth + open ethics.
ideas.claudePrompt: Draft README + LICENSE + data-card for Hugging Face dataset 'NOURISH-cultural-glycemic-atlas-v0.' Include schema, ethics statement, 3 example queries, citation entry. Save to /sous/labs/atlas/.

ideas.title: Sous-as-grant-data — package the app's telemetry
ideas.essay: Sous already collects food-recognition + craving + behavior data on consenting users. This is exactly the phenotype AHRQ R21/R33 is willing to fund — point-of-care digital tool with embedded outcome data. Reframe Sous from 'app' to 'research data acquisition platform' in next grant.
ideas.mechanism: Re-write Sous data flow doc to surface: every meal logged is N-of-1 micro-study; every coaching cycle is behavior-change event; every craving parse is intent capture. Attach to GR-001 narrative.
ideas.edge: Most app companies don't think of telemetry as research data; most academic groups don't have apps. Stefan does both.
ideas.claudePrompt: Open Sous /docs/Sous_whitepaper_PRD_v1.docx. Create sibling doc 'Sous as a Research Data Platform — AHRQ R21/R33 framing.' 1-page exec summary + 3-page research case + telemetry schema appendix. Save to /sous/docs/.

ideas.title: Plate Method 2.0 — generative plate composition
ideas.essay: ADA's Plate Method is iconic but static. With Sous's recognition stack you can ship a plate scanner that says 'half-plate veggies, quarter protein' — but in real time, with cuisine-correct examples. Plate Method 2.0 = picture-aware, cuisine-aware, immediate.
ideas.mechanism: Pipe Sous's existing food-recognition output into plate-area calculator + cuisine-rule overlay. Ship as feature inside Sous + as standalone PWA partnered with ADA.
seeds: Linda's plating principles from Le Cordon Bleu
ideas.edge: ADA wants to modernize without losing brand recognition. Plate Method 2.0 keeps the brand and adds AI without changing the symbol.
ideas.claudePrompt: Mock Sous screen: user takes plate photo, sees cuisine-aware quadrant overlay + score + nudge. Tailwind + Next.js. Save to /sous/components/today/plate-method-v2.tsx.

ideas.title: The 'Cuisine-Forward' Grant Cover Letter Template
ideas.essay: The Apr 28 media-strategy meeting decided to drop 'culturally tailored / equity-centered' in favor of 'cuisine-based / taste-forward.' Same reframe applies to grant cover letters. Write this template once, reuse across every NOURISH grant.
ideas.mechanism: Build 1-page boilerplate template + 3 grant-specific variants (AHRQ, PCORI, HRSA). Template auto-fills funder, amount, mechanism. Stash in Stefan-Brain/wiki/nourish/templates/.
ideas.impact: Medium — every grant we write benefits
ideas.edge: Templates compound — every reuse saves 30 min and improves consistency. This week the team needs at least 3 cover letters.
ideas.claudePrompt: Write 'NOURISH Cuisine-Forward Grant Cover Letter — Boilerplate v1.docx.' 4 sections: 1-paragraph NOURISH overview, 2-paragraph project alignment with funder priorities, mechanism + budget anchor, named-PI + collaborators paragraph. Variables in {{double curly}}. Save to wiki/nourish/templates/.

ideas.title: The Surry Hills Disability-Research Bridge
ideas.essay: Stefan returns to NSW disability policy 1.5 days/week. NOURISH has zero disability-research presence. There's a $13.7M NDRP grant ending Dec 2027 that could fund a disability-nutrition crossover study. Build the bridge while embedded both places.
ideas.mechanism: Pitch Louisa Ratz on disability-nutrition thesis: 'How does CGM coaching work for people with intellectual or physical disabilities who struggle with traditional dietary advice?' Use Stefan's mixed-methods background as entry credential.
seeds: Stefan's Aboriginal research + assistive tech evaluation background
ideas.edge: Stefan has both feet in the doorways. Nobody else has nutrition + AI + disability + Aboriginal research credentials.
ideas.claudePrompt: Draft 1-page concept note 'Disability-Aware Glycemic Coaching: NOURISH meets NDRP.' Sections: opportunity, proposed mechanism, Stefan's qualifications, ask (15-min meeting with Louisa). Email-formatted, 250 words.

ideas.title: MAHA grant as plain-language essay
ideas.essay: May 1 meeting flagged Linda's MAHA draft needs significant plain-language rewrite. Symptom of broader thesis: most NOURISH grants are over-academic for actual reviewers. Treat plain-language rewriting as workflow + asset, not one-off.
ideas.mechanism: Build plain-language rewriter prompt + workflow. Run every grant through it before submission. Audit 3 past grants retroactively to validate.
ideas.edge: Rewrite quality compounds. Each grant submitted in plain language increases the next one's quality through better reviewer feedback.
ideas.claudePrompt: Write Claude prompt template 'plain-language-grant-rewriter.md' that takes a paragraph and outputs the same content rewritten at 9th-grade reading level. Save to ~/.claude/prompts/.

ideas.title: Patient Handbook → Audiobook + Podcast pipeline
ideas.essay: Mehek + Reha have Patient Handbook in revision. Once stable, the same content can be re-rendered as audiobook + episodic podcast. ElevenLabs + Maya Adam connection makes this nearly free to produce.
ideas.mechanism: Stable handbook → ElevenLabs voice clone → 12-episode podcast structure. Maya Adam pitch is distribution. Health Library is host.
seeds: Maya Adam podcast outreach already in motion
Stanford Health Library video host decision
ideas.edge: Most academic groups produce handbooks. Few re-cut them as audio. Stefan + NOURISH have the AI infrastructure to do it without burning team hours.
ideas.claudePrompt: Outline 12-episode podcast series structure based on Patient Handbook. Each episode: title, 1-paragraph summary, 3 talking points, suggested guest. Save as 'NOURISH-podcast-season-1.md' in wiki/nourish/.

ideas.title: ADA Placemat 2.0 — the rewrite they want
ideas.essay: ADA explicitly rejected the block-format placemat. The fix is rewrite that's narrative-first, image-second. Design new placemat as story arc: meal scenarios → swap suggestions → confidence cue. Cuisine-forward framing per Apr 28 decision.
ideas.mechanism: Storyboard 3-panel placemat: scenario, swap, confidence. South Asian + Filipino versions. Mehek + Stephanie can ship in 5 days if narrative is locked.
seeds: ADA rewrite request from May 1 meeting
ideas.edge: We have the team + content + ADA relationship. The blocker is design narrative, not capacity.
ideas.claudePrompt: Storyboard 'ADA Placemat 2.0 — South Asian.' 3 panels: meal scenario (e.g., dosa breakfast), swap (paneer instead of potato filling), confidence cue (with this swap, your post-meal glucose stays under 140). Pair with Filipino version. Format: Markdown with image-prompt placeholders.

ideas.title: The 'NOURISH-in-a-Box' Clinic Onboarding Kit
ideas.essay: Cupertino obesity-MD wants NOURISH in her clinic. Right now there's no onboarding kit. Build one once: video series + plate method poster + recipe booklet + 1-page partner agreement. Reusable for every future clinic partnership inquiry.
ideas.mechanism: Bundle existing assets into single Notion-published kit. 1-page partner agreement is only new artifact. Anyone on team can deploy.
seeds: Cupertino MD partnership inquiry from May 1
Existing NOURISH video series + recipe booklets
ideas.impact: High — scales clinic partnerships without team scale
ideas.edge: Stefan + Dr. Moharir's most-asked question from external clinics is 'what does it take to bring NOURISH to my practice?' Right now answer is 'we'll figure it out.' This makes it 'click here.'
ideas.claudePrompt: Outline 'NOURISH-in-a-Box: Clinic Onboarding Kit v1.' Cover page + 5 contents: 1-page program description, video links, plate-method poster (PDF), recipe booklet (PDF), 1-page partner MOU. Save to wiki/nourish/clinic-kit/.

ideas.title: Cookbook as Stanford Press monograph play
ideas.essay: Once nutrition labels are validated (Stephanie + Dahlia), NOURISH cookbook can be pitched to Stanford Press as monograph rather than self-published. Stanford Press = academic credibility + Stanford bookstore distribution + perpetual royalty stream.
ideas.mechanism: Identify Stanford Press editor + format for monograph submission. Reframe cookbook from consumer to academic-with-recipes.
ideas.edge: Most cookbook teams target Penguin Random House. Going Stanford Press first is academic-credibility-first, which compounds with grant strategy.
ideas.claudePrompt: Draft 1-page Stanford Press inquiry email + 1-page monograph proposal. Sections: working title, scope, audience, comp titles, author qualifications, evidence base. Save to wiki/nourish/cookbook/.

ideas.title: Sous + GLP-1 user — the next research cohort
ideas.essay: GLP-1 users don't get good nutrition coaching. Sous + GLP-1 user cohort is the next obvious research expansion — they have measurable outcomes (weight + glycemic), monitored adherence, and an ROI window.
ideas.mechanism: Recruit 30 GLP-1 users via Cupertino MD partner + Stanford clinics. Run Sous coaching for 12 weeks. Outcome: glycemic + qualitative satisfaction. Pitch to NIH NIDDK Digital Health for T2D (GR-006).
seeds: GR-006 NIH Digital Health for T2D
ideas.edge: GLP-1 nutrition coaching is the topic of 2026. Stefan can run a tight 12-week study faster than incumbent GLP-1 lifestyle apps.
ideas.claudePrompt: Outline 1-page R21 specific aims for 'Sous AI Coaching for GLP-1 Users: Glycemic and Behavioral Outcomes at 12 Weeks.' Standard 3-aim structure. Save to wiki/nourish/grants/.

ideas.title: Health Trust $5K micro-grant pilot
ideas.essay: Dr. Moharir flagged Health Trust on May 1 — they fund $5K small grants for community food/nutrition work. Tiny effort to apply, fast turnaround, easy citation. Use as 'first win' before larger HRSA-tier grants.
ideas.mechanism: Apply for $5K Health Trust grant. Use proceeds to fund one targeted asset (e.g., AI-app development microsprint or cohort recruitment cycle).
seeds: Granola May 1 mention of Health Trust + Teresa Johnson
ideas.edge: Quick wins generate momentum. Easy grants are referenceable in larger applications.
ideas.claudePrompt: Draft 1-page Health Trust LOI with 250-word program description, $5K budget breakdown, named-PI paragraph. Save to wiki/nourish/grants/lois/.

ideas.title: The Disability × Nutrition adjacency thesis paper
ideas.essay: Disability + nutrition is under-researched intersection. Stefan's two-track positioning (NSW disability + NOURISH) lets him write the canonical adjacency paper. Once cited, future grants in either domain reference it.
ideas.mechanism: Lit-review week. Write 5-page perspective paper. Submit to Disability and Health Journal or American Journal of Public Health.
ideas.impact: Medium — citation engine for next grants
ideas.edge: Adjacency papers are easier to publish than RCTs and have asymmetric citation upside.
ideas.claudePrompt: Outline 5-page perspective paper 'Disability-Aware Digital Nutrition Coaching: An Underexplored Intersection.' 4 sections: scope of gap, why now, three case studies, call to research. Save to wiki/research/papers/.

ideas.title: Sous Sommelier × AHA Statin Misinformation Bridge
ideas.essay: Two Bumblebee threads converge: misinformation pairing engine + AHA partnership via Ankita. Convergence: AHA wants better statin compliance content, NOURISH cuisine-forward framing fits, Sous can deliver pairing-aware misinformation rebuttal.
ideas.mechanism: Joint NOURISH-Ankita pitch to AHA: 'cuisine-forward statin support.' Pair statin myths with traditional-food rebuttals. Test in NOURISH cohort.
ideas.edge: Cross-org joint pitches are hard for big orgs but easy for small ones to assemble. NOURISH + Ankita = first mover.
ideas.claudePrompt: Draft 1-page joint pitch deck (5 slides) 'Cuisine-Forward Statin Support: NOURISH × Ankita Patel × AHA.' Save as Markdown with slide-break markers. Save to wiki/nourish/joint-pitches/.

ideas.title: Operation Coach — a CGM-aware Operation game for med students
ideas.essay: If clinical-signs-game is the flagship tinker, Operation Coach is the next: a board-game style learning tool where med students place coaching interventions on a virtual patient and watch CGM curves respond. Operation meets clinical reasoning meets behavioral nutrition.
ideas.mechanism: Cartoony patient with CGM trace; player intervenes (food swap, exercise, GLP-1 dose) and sees curve update; data captured = which interventions students reach for. Research-by-accident: 'what do future docs miss when CGM coaching gets gamified?'
ideas.edge: Med-ed games haven't been hit by digital biomarkers. CGM coaching education hasn't been gamified. The combination is virgin territory.
ideas.claudePrompt: Scaffold Next.js + SVG project 'operation-coach.' One screen: patient body, CGM curve at top, intervention chips at bottom. Click chip → curve animates. Save to /tinker/operation-coach/.

ideas.name: Operation Coach
ideas.tagline: Operation board game, but each click adjusts a CGM glucose curve.
ideas.mechanic: Tap intervention chip → cartoony patient's CGM trace updates in real time.
ideas.researchAngle: Captures which interventions med students reach for first; surfaces gaps in CGM coaching education.
ideas.claudePrompt: Scaffold Next.js + Tailwind + Framer Motion. One page. Cartoony body SVG with CGM trace at top. 6 intervention chips at bottom (food swap, exercise, GLP-1, hydration, sleep, none). Click → animate CGM trace. Use placeholder physiology. Save to /tinker/operation-coach/.

ideas.name: FlavorBingo
ideas.tagline: 5-minute weekly bingo card of culturally-paired flavor combinations to try.
ideas.mechanic: Spin → bingo card of 9 flavor pairs (FlavorDB-derived); tick what you cook this week.
ideas.researchAngle: Tracks which cross-cuisine pairings users actually try; feeds Sous recommendation engine.
ideas.claudePrompt: One-page Next.js. On load, randomly pick 9 flavor pairs from hardcoded FlavorDB sample. Show as 3x3 bingo grid with tap-to-mark-done. Add 'spin again Monday' message. Save to /tinker/flavorbingo/.

ideas.name: GlucoSimulator
ideas.tagline: Predict the glucose curve from a recipe photo before you eat — in 10 seconds.
ideas.mechanic: Upload photo → see predicted CGM curve overlay → eat → upload real CGM data → score your prediction.
ideas.researchAngle: Trains people to predict their own glycemic response; surfaces individual prediction errors that inform coaching.
ideas.claudePrompt: Next.js: upload photo → call Sous /predict → render predicted CGM curve via Recharts. Then upload-real-CGM input → overlay both curves + show prediction-error score. Save to /tinker/glucosimulator/.

ideas.name: RecipeHaiku
ideas.tagline: Reduce any recipe to a haiku that captures cuisine, technique, and flavor in 17 syllables.
ideas.mechanic: Paste recipe → AI generates haiku → user taps Like or Re-Roll.
ideas.researchAngle: Captures what humans rate as 'true to the recipe' — useful signal for Sous craving-parser quality.
ideas.claudePrompt: Single-page Next.js. Paste textarea → Claude API call with system prompt 'Output exactly one haiku capturing the recipe's cuisine, primary technique, and flavor.' Render output + Like/Re-Roll. Save to /tinker/recipehaiku/.

ideas.name: PlateRoulette
ideas.tagline: Spin to get tonight's dinner: cuisine + protein + side, all from your CGM-friendly bank.
ideas.mechanic: Big spin button → 3-slot result (cuisine, protein, side); tap a slot to re-roll just that one.
ideas.researchAngle: Captures which combinations users keep vs. re-roll — passive preference signal for Sous.
ideas.claudePrompt: Single-page Next.js. Big spin button. On click, animate 3 slots. Hardcoded options for cuisine/protein/side. Each slot tappable to re-roll. Save to /tinker/plateroulette/.

ideas.name: CuisineSeeker
ideas.tagline: Like Pokémon GO for cuisines — walk to a restaurant, log a dish, level up the cuisine card.
ideas.mechanic: Geolocation → nearest restaurant → photo log → cuisine XP → cuisine card progresses.
ideas.techStack: Next.js + Geolocation API + Leaflet for map
ideas.researchAngle: Maps real-world cuisine consumption patterns by neighborhood — useful for NOURISH cohort framing.
ideas.claudePrompt: Next.js mobile-first PWA. Use browser Geolocation → query Overpass API for nearby restaurants → user picks one + uploads dish photo → award XP to cuisine card. Save to /tinker/cuisineseeker/.

ideas.name: SymphonyOfMeals
ideas.tagline: Each ingredient becomes a musical note — your shopping cart plays a song.
ideas.mechanic: Add ingredient → assigned a Tone.js note → cart plays melody when checked out; export to MIDI.
ideas.researchAngle: Captures emotional resonance of meal compositions — orthogonal data for Sous recommendation.
ideas.claudePrompt: Next.js + Tone.js. Type ingredient → mapped to note via hash. Build cart list. Click 'Play cart' → plays melody in sequence. Add MIDI export. Save to /tinker/symphony-of-meals/.

ideas.name: GlossaryGarden
ideas.tagline: A garden where each plant is a nutrition term you've learned this week.
ideas.mechanic: Read NOURISH micro-lesson → unlock plant → garden grows; share screenshot.
ideas.researchAngle: Tracks which terms get shared most — content traction signal.
ideas.claudePrompt: Next.js + Tailwind + SVG. Garden grid (4x4). Each cell is plant SVG, locked by default. Mini-lesson modal unlocks each. Persist via localStorage. Save to /tinker/glossarygarden/.

ideas.name: DiseaseDispatch
ideas.tagline: PagerDuty for chronic disease: gamified clinical-decision dispatching for med students.
ideas.mechanic: Patient case pings in (cardiac, T2D) → student picks intervention pathway → score on outcome + cost.
ideas.researchAngle: Records what pathways novice clinicians choose; what they miss.
ideas.claudePrompt: Next.js. Pager-style alert UI. New 'patient' shows case summary. Student picks from 4 pathways. Score reveal screen. Add 'next case' loop. Save to /tinker/diseasedispatch/.

ideas.name: WellnessWordle
ideas.tagline: Wordle, but the daily word is a nutrition term and you learn its etymology after solving.
ideas.mechanic: Standard Wordle 5-letter mechanic; reveal screen includes 30-second etymology lesson.
ideas.researchAngle: Tracks daily streaks; captures which terms drive repeat plays.
ideas.claudePrompt: Single-page Next.js. Wordle clone with curated 200-word nutrition list. Reveal screen includes etymology + 1-line clinical relevance. Save to /tinker/wellnesswordle/.

ideas.name: RhythmEater
ideas.tagline: Tap food onto the plate in time with the beat — physics-based plate composition game.
ideas.mechanic: Music drives spawning; user taps food onto plate; physics keeps half-plate veggies; score on visual + nutrition balance.
ideas.researchAngle: Embodied learning of plate-method principles; tap-pattern data shows where the rule breaks down.
ideas.claudePrompt: Next.js + Matter.js for plate physics + Tone.js for beat. Spawn food items in sync; user taps to drop them on plate. Show score with plate-method rule alignment. Save to /tinker/rhythmeater/.

ideas.name: MicroMonologue
ideas.tagline: 60-second voice diary on what you ate — Sous transcribes + scores it.
ideas.mechanic: Hold-to-record → release → transcript + auto-extracted ingredient list + glycemic risk pill.
ideas.techStack: Next.js + Web Speech API + Sous craving-parser
ideas.researchAngle: Voice-first food diaries; captures effort + descriptive richness, useful for Sous coaching context.
ideas.claudePrompt: Next.js. Hold-to-record button using Web Speech API. On release: transcript shown + Sous craving-parser API call → ingredient list + glycemic-risk pill. Save to /tinker/micromonologue/.

ideas.name: PostureCheck
ideas.tagline: Sit up — Sous reminds you with a 5-second breathing animation every 25 minutes.
ideas.mechanic: Background timer fires → soft sound + on-screen breathing circle → tap to dismiss.
ideas.techStack: Next.js PWA + Service Worker + Notification API
ideas.researchAngle: Tracks dismissal patterns; reveals which times of day reminders are stickiest.
ideas.claudePrompt: Next.js PWA. Service worker schedules notification every 25 min. Tapping notification opens full-screen breathing animation (5 inhale, 5 hold, 5 exhale). Save to /tinker/posturecheck/.

ideas.name: RecipeRoulette-VR
ideas.tagline: Three random recipes appear in a 3D wheel — flick to spin, point to pick.
ideas.mechanic: Three.js 3D wheel with recipe cards; flick to rotate; tap to choose; share rotation as GIF.
ideas.researchAngle: Captures dwell-time on each recipe option — proxy for desirability.
ideas.claudePrompt: Next.js + Three.js. 3D rotating wheel with 3 recipe cards as textures. Flick gesture rotates; tap selects; export rotation as GIF. Save to /tinker/recipe-roulette-vr/.

ideas.name: DiabetesDuel
ideas.tagline: Two-player blood-glucose strategy game: pick meals + activity to keep AUC low for 24h.
ideas.mechanic: Each player picks 24-hour meal+activity plan; simulated AUC computed; lowest AUC wins.
ideas.researchAngle: Captures which strategies people invent for AUC management; useful for coaching content.
ideas.claudePrompt: Next.js. Two-player setup. Each picks 3 meals + 1 activity. Run simple AUC simulator (placeholder physiology). Show winner + breakdown. Save to /tinker/diabetesduel/.

ideas.name: ClinicCompass
ideas.tagline: Find the nearest culturally-aligned dietitian with one tap.
ideas.mechanic: Geolocation → list of RDs filtered by language + cuisine + insurance; tap to call.
ideas.techStack: Next.js + Geolocation + curated RD directory JSON
ideas.researchAngle: Captures demand patterns for cuisine-aligned RDs; informs NOURISH partner recruitment.
ideas.claudePrompt: Next.js. Geolocation → query directory JSON → list with filters (language, cuisine, insurance). Tap-to-call (tel:). Save to /tinker/cliniccompass/.

ideas.name: SpiceShelf
ideas.tagline: Photograph your spice shelf — get 7 dinner ideas for the week.
ideas.mechanic: Photo → CV identifies spices → AI proposes 7 cuisine-aware dinners using only those spices.
ideas.researchAngle: Captures real-world spice inventories; useful for Sous personalization.
ideas.claudePrompt: Next.js. Upload photo → call Sous food-recognition (tuned for spice jars) → list extracted spices → Claude API for 7 dinner ideas. Save to /tinker/spiceshelf/.

ideas.name: WhistleStop
ideas.tagline: When you start cooking, the screen becomes a 3-min hourglass — no notifications get through.
ideas.mechanic: Tap 'cooking now' → fullscreen hourglass + Do-Not-Disturb. After 3 min, soft chime + photo prompt.
ideas.researchAngle: Captures cooking session lengths; benchmarks 'when do people give up?'
ideas.claudePrompt: Next.js PWA. Single button 'cooking now.' Activates wake-lock + fullscreen hourglass animation. After 3 min, chime + photo upload prompt. Save to /tinker/whistlestop/.

ideas.name: CGMComic
ideas.tagline: Your day's CGM trace becomes a 4-panel comic with cartoonish meal characters.
ideas.mechanic: Upload CGM CSV → app extracts meal events → renders 4-panel comic with cartoon-style meal characters.
ideas.techStack: Next.js + SVG comic generation + Claude for caption
ideas.researchAngle: Comics are highly shareable — turns CGM data into social content; tracks which days users export.
ideas.claudePrompt: Next.js. CSV upload → identify meal events (peak detection) → generate 4 SVG panels with cartoon characters per meal + Claude API for funny captions. Save to /tinker/cgmcomic/.

ideas.name: MealMimic
ideas.tagline: Pick a chef's signature dish — Sous tells you what's in your kitchen that approximates it.
ideas.mechanic: Pick chef + dish → Sous compares ingredient list to your pantry photo → returns delta + substitution map.
ideas.techStack: Next.js + Sous food-recognition + ingredient knowledge base
ideas.researchAngle: Captures substitution patterns; useful for Sous coaching content.
ideas.claudePrompt: Next.js. Dropdown: chef + dish. Pantry-photo upload. Compute ingredient delta + substitution suggestions via Claude. Save to /tinker/mealmimic/.

candidates.name: all-grants-doc-writer
candidates.pattern: Stefan repeatedly compiles cross-grant Word docs. 3+ instances in last 30 days.
candidates.action: Skill that pulls Airtable Submitted Proposals + Grant Scout, generates Word doc grouped by status with 1-line description per grant.
candidates.evidence: wiki/_log/ + May 1 Granola transcript explicit ask

candidates.name: plain-language-grant-rewriter
candidates.pattern: Linda's MAHA grant + past PCORI drafts — repeated 'rewrite for plain language' cycle.
candidates.action: Claude prompt template that takes any grant paragraph and outputs 9th-grade-reading-level rewrite while preserving technical claims.
candidates.evidence: Granola May 1 + Apr 28 Linda meeting summary

candidates.name: nourish-clinic-onboarding-kit-deploy
candidates.pattern: Cupertino MD inquiry + future inquiries — every clinic ask requires same artifact bundle.
candidates.action: Skill that, given clinic name + cuisine, generates partner MOU, video link bundle, recipe booklet, personalized cover note.
candidates.evidence: May 1 Granola — Cupertino MD partnership ask

candidates.name: weekly-airtable-task-triage
candidates.pattern: 47 tasks Not Started/In Progress overdue from Feb–March; weekly review accumulates drag.
candidates.action: Skill that scans Task Registry weekly, groups overdue tasks by recommended action (Close / Push / Kill), proposes Airtable updates for batch approval.
candidates.evidence: Airtable list_records_for_table — 138 records, 51 overdue Not Started

candidates.name: granola-to-airtable-task-sync
candidates.pattern: Every Granola meeting yields tasks; Stefan currently transcribes manually.
candidates.action: Skill polls new Granola meetings, extracts action items, runs them through SOP, creates Airtable records in batch.
candidates.evidence: wiki/_log/ — repeated invocations of airtable-task-registry-hygiene-sop in Granola mode

candidates.name: grant-deadline-radar
candidates.pattern: Stefan misses grant deadlines (Apr 28 PCORI LOI was last-minute). Want 14-day-out daily nudge.
candidates.action: Daily scheduled task scans grant-registry.md, surfaces grants with deadlines in 14 days, posts Slack/Granola summary.
candidates.evidence: 82 grants in registry; 12 active with imminent deadlines

candidates.name: cuisine-forward-language-auditor
candidates.pattern: Apr 28 decision: every NOURISH artifact needs cuisine-forward audit before public.
candidates.action: Skill scans asset (PDF, doc, slide deck) for 'culturally tailored / equity-centered' language; proposes cuisine-forward replacements.

candidates.name: video-podcast-pipeline
candidates.pattern: NOURISH videos exist but aren't promoted; cookbook + handbook content not yet repurposed.
candidates.action: Skill takes any NOURISH content asset and proposes 3 derivative formats (podcast episode, social clip, blog post) with copy-paste scripts.

candidates.name: intro-pack-mailer
candidates.pattern: Stefan sends NOURISH intro pack to multiple new contacts every week.
candidates.action: Skill that, given contact + context, generates personalized 4-line email + attachment bundle, saves as draft in Outlook.
candidates.evidence: Sylvia, Isabella, Ankita, Cupertino MD — 4 in this week alone

candidates.name: stefan-brain-log-auto-rollup
candidates.pattern: Daily logs accumulate; weekly synthesis is manual.
candidates.action: Skill reads wiki/_log/ for week, produces 5-bullet weekly synthesis, posts to wiki/_log/_weekly/.
candidates.evidence: Stefan-Brain CLAUDE.md Law 5 + this run's manual synthesis

candidates.name: mtm-roi-shortcut-tracker
candidates.pattern: Mona's research thread + GR-080 CMS RHT alignment is high-leverage thread that needs ongoing tracking.
candidates.action: Skill watches Mona's outputs + CMS publications + Sous CGM data; surfaces actionable insights monthly.
candidates.evidence: May 1 Granola + GR-080 from prior week's grant scan

candidates.name: sous-strategy-doc-auditor
candidates.pattern: Sous CLAUDE.md mandates STRATEGY.md consultation before features; reality is occasional drift.
candidates.action: Pre-commit hook + skill that, on any feature commit, asks 'does this serve strategic thesis?' and refuses commits without 1-line answer.

candidates.name: tinker-deploy-wizard
candidates.pattern: Stefan wants to ship many small tinker projects. Friction = setting up Vercel + Next.js boilerplate every time.
candidates.action: Skill that, given tinker name + 1-line description, scaffolds Next.js + Tailwind + Vercel-ready repo with README and deploys it.
candidates.evidence: Tinker tab has 20 ideas; wiki/tinker shows only 2 active builds

candidates.name: collaborator-touch-cadence-monitor
candidates.pattern: Sylvia (3 days late) + Razor + Granpadilla + 4 others — weekly cadence drift accumulates.
candidates.action: Daily skill scans Collaborators table for Last Touch + Touch Cadence; surfaces overdue contacts as 5-name daily nudge.
